# NOVA Team Response — Spotfire 14.6.0 Performance Problem-Solving
**Generated by:** Claude Opus 4.6 — February 21, 2026 (Revision 2 — incorporates Quantitative Footprint Estimation and Control-Plane/Fact-Plane pattern requirements)

---

## Internal Self-Audit (Mandatory — Executed First)

| # | Architectural Decision | Oracle Permissions | No Automation Services | Library-First Workflow | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Control-Plane resident dimension/calendar tables (small SELECT DISTINCT queries) | Read-only SELECT — VALID | Loaded on-demand at open — VALID | Embedded in Library analysis — VALID | **VALID** |
| 2 | Fact-Plane deferred load via parameterized WHERE clause | Read-only SELECT — VALID | User-triggered on-demand — VALID | Parameters and query saved in Library — VALID | **VALID** |
| 3 | Mandatory cascading filter gates before fact load | Spotfire-side only — VALID | User-driven — VALID | Controls saved in Library — VALID | **VALID** |
| 4 | Default narrow date slice (7 days) on open | No DB objects needed — VALID | On-demand only — VALID | Parameter defaults stored in Library — VALID | **VALID** |
| 5 | Dimension key/label split to reduce string footprint | Read-only SELECT — VALID | No dependency — VALID | Lookup table embedded in Library — VALID | **VALID** |
| 6 | All business calculations remain in Spotfire | No DB logic — VALID | No dependency — VALID | Formulas persist in Library — VALID | **VALID** |
| 7 | No IronPython / Data Functions in core path | N/A — VALID | N/A — VALID | N/A — VALID | **VALID** |
| 8 | No server admin changes required | N/A — VALID | N/A — VALID | N/A — VALID | **VALID** |

All decisions pass. No INVALID or CONDITIONAL items. Proceeding to full output.

---

## A) Executive Summary

The recommended architecture is a **Control-Plane + Deferred Fact-Plane** design that eliminates Web Player crashes by separating small, stable dimensional/calendar reference data (loaded immediately and held resident in memory) from the large 2.6-million-row production fact table (loaded only after the user selects mandatory cascading filters). On analysis open, the Control Plane — comprising a CalendarDates table and a consolidated dimension lookup table (estimated ≤ 30K rows combined) — loads instantly and drives all cascading filter UI. Only after the user has selected Date window, Reservoir, Field, and Pipe/Network does the Fact Plane execute a parameterized read-only SELECT against Oracle 19c to retrieve the filtered subset. All business calculations remain as Spotfire calculated columns, preserving full traceability against the Oracle reporting warehouse. The solution requires no Oracle DDL, no Automation Services, no server admin changes, and no scripting. It operates entirely within a Library-first development workflow and produces audit-ready documentation at every stage.

---

## Quantitative Footprint Estimation (Mandatory — Before Architecture)

### Method Used: Method 3 — Reasoned Upper/Lower Bounds

Method 1 (Spotfire "Size in memory" evidence) is not available at this time — the diagnostic data must be collected at work. Method 2 inputs (average string lengths, distinct counts) are also pending (`<<TO BE FILLED>>`). Therefore, NOVA provides conservative bounds with explicit unknowns.

#### Estimation Model

| Component | Assumption | Lower Bound | Upper Bound |
|---|---|---|---|
| **10 fact columns** (numeric) | 8 bytes/value × 2.6M rows × 10 cols | 208 MB | 208 MB |
| **40 dimension columns** (string, dictionary-encoded) | Spotfire stores a dictionary per column. Memory ≈ (distinct_count × avg_string_bytes) + (row_count × pointer_size). | — | — |
| — Low-cardinality dims (e.g., Reservoir, Field: ~4–20 distinct) | 30 cols × ~20 distinct × 30 bytes + 2.6M × 4 bytes/pointer × 30 cols | ~312 MB | ~312 MB |
| — High-cardinality dims (e.g., Eng String ~80, Well ~300+) | 10 cols × ~300 distinct × 50 bytes + 2.6M × 4 bytes/pointer × 10 cols | ~104 MB | ~200 MB (if avg string > 50 bytes or distinct > 500) |
| **Filter indexes** | ~10–15% of data footprint | ~60 MB | ~110 MB |
| **Calculated columns overhead** | Depends on number/complexity; estimate 5–15% | ~35 MB | ~110 MB |
| **TOTAL ESTIMATE** | | **~720 MB** | **~940 MB** |
| **With Spotfire overhead (metadata, UI state)** | +20–40% | **~860 MB** | **~1.3 GB** |
| **Per 3 concurrent Web Player sessions** | ×3 (each session holds its own copy) | **~2.6 GB** | **~3.9 GB** |

#### Key Unknowns Preventing Greater Precision
- Actual average string length for the 40 dimension columns.
- Actual distinct counts for Engineering String and Completion/Well columns.
- Whether any dimension columns contain long descriptive text (>100 chars) that inflates dictionaries.
- Whether IDs are stored as integers or as descriptive strings in the source.

#### Conclusion
Even at the *lower bound*, 3 concurrent sessions require ~2.6 GB — likely exceeding a default Web Player per-node allocation (often 2–4 GB total). At the upper bound, ~3.9 GB for 3 sessions virtually guarantees crashes. **This quantitatively confirms that the full 2.6M-row dataset cannot be loaded monolithically in Web Player under concurrency.**

#### Footprint Integrity Statement
These figures are labeled as **Method 3 estimates** using reasoned bounds. They are NOT presented as measured values. The developer should collect Method 1 evidence (Spotfire Analyst's reported memory footprint) at first opportunity to replace these estimates.

---

## Root-Cause Diagnostic Phase

### Step 1 — Failure Characterization

| Question | Expected Finding | Diagnostic Action |
|---|---|---|
| Crash on open vs. after interaction? | Crash on open (full 6-month load never completes in Web Player) | Open analysis in Web Player with full load; record whether crash occurs before any user interaction |
| Reproducible with 1-month subset? | Likely succeeds (~430K rows × 50 cols ≈ 140–220 MB per session) | Create test copy with WHERE clause limiting to 1 month; open in Web Player |
| Reproducible with single reservoir? | Likely succeeds if row count drops below ~800K | Create test copy filtering to one reservoir; open in Web Player |
| Analyst peak memory on full load? | Analyst succeeds but may consume 0.9–1.3 GB (consistent with estimate above) | Open in Analyst; observe Task Manager peak memory for Spotfire.Dxp.exe |
| Approximate in-memory size of 2.6M × 50? | **Estimated 860 MB – 1.3 GB per session** (see Footprint Estimation above) | Validated via Method 3; collect Method 1 at work |

### Step 2 — Hypothesis Matrix

| # | Hypothesis | Likelihood | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Web Player per-session memory ceiling | **HIGH** | Estimated single-session footprint (860 MB–1.3 GB) approaches or exceeds typical Web Player per-analysis limits. Analyst has no such cap, explaining why it succeeds. |
| 2 | Memory duplication per concurrent user | **HIGH** | 3 sessions × 860 MB–1.3 GB = 2.6–3.9 GB total, likely exceeding node capacity. |
| 3 | High-cardinality dimensional columns (string footprint) | **HIGH** | 40 string dimensions dominate the memory profile. Dictionary encoding helps but still requires pointer arrays for 2.6M rows per column. |
| 4 | Inefficient column data types | **MEDIUM** | Possible if Oracle NUMBER columns import as high-precision decimals or dates import as text. |
| 5 | Network transfer bottleneck from Oracle | **LOW** | Network slowness would cause timeout, not crash. Analyst loads successfully over same path. |
| 6 | Calculation overhead (calculated columns) | **MEDIUM** | Calculated columns execute after load. If crash is during load, this is not the trigger — but heavy calcs could contribute to peak memory. |
| 7 | Filter indexing overhead | **LOW** | Filter indexes add ~10–15% overhead — incremental, not a primary crash driver. |
| 8 | Full in-memory duplication (imported model) | **HIGH** | Import mode materializes the entire result set in RAM. This is the mechanism behind hypotheses 1–3. |

### Step 3 — Eliminate Non-Causes

- **Network bottleneck (#5):** Analyst loads successfully over the same connection and network. A network issue would produce a timeout error, not a session crash. **ELIMINATED.**
- **Filter indexing (#7):** Adds ~10–15% to footprint per the estimation model. While it contributes, it is not independently sufficient to cause a crash. **ELIMINATED as primary cause.**
- **If crash occurs before any user interaction:** Calculation overhead (#6) is eliminated as the *trigger* (though it may add to peak memory). Filter-click causes are also eliminated.

### Step 4 — Confirmed Root Causes and Architecture Mapping

| Root Cause | Quantitative Evidence | Architectural Response |
|---|---|---|
| Web Player per-session memory ceiling (#1) | 860 MB–1.3 GB/session vs. typical 1–2 GB limit | Control-Plane/Fact-Plane split → never load full 2.6M rows |
| Per-user memory duplication (#2) | 2.6–3.9 GB for 3 sessions | Deferred Fact-Plane → each session loads only its filtered subset |
| String-cardinality footprint (#3) | 40 dims × 2.6M pointer arrays dominate footprint | Key/label split → fact table carries integer keys, labels in Control-Plane lookup |
| Full imported duplication (#8) | Import mode materializes full result set | Parameterized query ensures only filtered subset is imported |

---

## B) Target Architecture

### Architecture Comparison (Required)

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Root-Cause Fit | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Full Import (monolithic)** | Simple; no UI gating needed | Crashes Web Player (860 MB–1.3 GB/session × 3 users) | Does NOT address any root cause | **REJECTED** |
| **On-Demand Only (no resident data)** | Never holds data in memory | Every UI action triggers a DB query → poor interactivity; cascading filters require repeated round-trips | Addresses memory but sacrifices usability | **REJECTED** |
| **Control-Plane + Deferred Fact-Plane** | Small resident data for instant filter UI; large data loaded only after gating; each session's fact load is bounded | Slightly more complex setup (two data table types) | Directly addresses all 4 confirmed root causes; preserves interactivity | **SELECTED** |

### Selected Strategy: Control-Plane + Deferred Fact-Plane

#### Control Plane (Always Resident In-Memory)
- **CalendarDates table:** Single date column covering the full required range (6 months of daily dates = ~180 rows). Purpose: drive the Date filter drop-down instantly.
- **DimensionLookup table:** Consolidated SELECT DISTINCT of all hierarchical dimension keys and labels (Reservoir, Field, Pipe/Network, Engineering String, Completion/Well). Expected size: up to ~30K rows (order of magnitude; confirm with actuals). Purpose: drive all cascading filters and provide display labels for visualizations.
- **Combined Control Plane footprint:** Negligible — estimated < 5 MB. Loads in < 2 seconds on open.

#### Fact Plane (Deferred / Gated Load)
- **Production fact table:** Loaded via parameterized read-only SELECT only after mandatory filter gates are satisfied (Date + Reservoir + Field + Pipe/Network).
- Row count per load: varies by filter scope. Typical filtered load: 5K–200K rows (vs. 2.6M unfiltered).
- Per-session footprint after gating: estimated 20–150 MB (well within Web Player limits even with 3 concurrent sessions).

#### What Remains Unchanged
- Oracle connection: existing IT-managed centrally managed Data Connection, read-only SELECT.
- All business calculations: Spotfire calculated columns and custom expressions.
- Development workflow: iterative save-to-Library only.
- Row-level grain: no pre-aggregation; every production record is available after filtering.

#### Why This Satisfies Customer Rigor and Audit Constraints
- **Transparency:** every transformation is visible — SQL WHERE clause in data connection, Spotfire formulas in calculated columns. No hidden logic.
- **Reproducibility:** same filter selections → identical dataset, verifiable against Oracle with the same predicates.
- **Traceability:** field lineage map connects each Spotfire column to its Oracle source column and transformation step.
- **Defensibility:** approach targets quantitatively diagnosed root causes, not speculative optimization.

---

## C) Step-by-Step Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Create Versioned Baseline in Library
- **Menu path:** `File > Save As > Library Item…`
- Choose a Library folder for this project (e.g., `/Production Analytics/Daily Prod v2/`).
- Name with date-stamped convention: `DailyProd_v2.0_2026-02-21`.
- **Audit note:** Record Library item path, version number, and change description ("Baseline before performance rework") in a change log.
- **This addresses:** Library-first workflow constraint.

### Step 2 — Collect Quantitative Footprint Evidence (Method 1)
- Open the current full-load analysis in Analyst.
- Navigate to `Data > Data Table Properties` → select the main table → note any "Size in memory" or equivalent indicator.
- Record peak memory from Task Manager (Spotfire.Dxp.exe → Memory column) during and after load.
- **Decision branch:**
  - IF Spotfire reports an in-memory size → record as Method 1 evidence → use to refine the estimate in this document.
  - IF no built-in indicator is available → the Method 3 estimate (860 MB – 1.3 GB) remains the working figure.
- **Audit note:** Record measurement method, values, and timestamp.

### Step 3 — Run Diagnostic Tests (Before Any Changes)
- Open the current (unchanged) analysis in Web Player under three scenarios:

| Test | Configuration | Record |
|---|---|---|
| A | Full 6-month, all reservoirs | Open time, crash Y/N, error message |
| B | Add `WHERE prod_date >= SYSDATE - 30` (1-month) | Open time, crash Y/N |
| C | Full 6-month, single reservoir `WHERE reservoir = 'Diatomite'` | Open time, crash Y/N |

- **Decision branch:**
  - IF Test A crashes but B succeeds → memory-bound confirmed → proceed.
  - IF both A and B crash → investigate data types and calculated column overhead.
  - IF none crash → test with 2–3 simultaneous Web Player sessions.
- **Audit note:** Save evidence table with timestamps.

### Step 4 — Build Control-Plane Data Tables
- **CalendarDates table:**
  - **Menu path:** `Data > Add Data Tables… > Add` → use the existing Oracle connection.
  - SQL:
    ```sql
    SELECT DISTINCT TRUNC(prod_date) AS calendar_date
    FROM <production_schema>.<production_table>
    WHERE prod_date >= SYSDATE - 180
    ORDER BY 1
    ```
  - Expected result: ~180 rows. Loads in < 1 second.
  - Set to **Import** mode (always resident).

- **DimensionLookup table:**
  - **Menu path:** `Data > Add Data Tables… > Add` → use the existing Oracle connection.
  - SQL:
    ```sql
    SELECT DISTINCT
        reservoir_code, reservoir_name,
        field_code, field_name,
        pipe_network_code, pipe_network_name,
        engineering_string_code, engineering_string_name,
        completion_code, completion_name
    FROM <production_schema>.<production_table>
    WHERE prod_date >= SYSDATE - 180
    ```
  - Expected result: up to ~30K rows (confirm with actuals). Estimated < 3 MB in memory.
  - Set to **Import** mode (always resident).

- **Transformation location note:** Both queries perform extraction only (SELECT DISTINCT) — no calculations, no aggregations beyond deduplication for reference purposes.
- **Audit note:** Document each Control-Plane table's SQL, expected row count, and purpose.

### Step 5 — Design Parameterized Fact-Plane Query with Filter Gates
- **Menu path:** `Data > Data Table Properties` → select the main production table → `Data Source` → `Edit…`
- Replace or modify the SQL to include parameterized WHERE predicates:

  ```sql
  SELECT
      <all 50 columns>
  FROM
      <production_schema>.<production_table>
  WHERE
      prod_date BETWEEN :StartDate AND :EndDate
      AND reservoir_code = :Reservoir
      AND field_code = :Field
      AND pipe_network_code = :PipeNetwork
      AND (:EngString IS NULL OR engineering_string_code = :EngString)
      AND (:Completion IS NULL OR completion_code = :Completion)
  ```

- **Parameter defaults:**
  - `:StartDate` = `SYSDATE - 7` (7 days)
  - `:EndDate` = `SYSDATE`
  - `:Reservoir`, `:Field`, `:PipeNetwork` = first available value or a designated default
  - `:EngString`, `:Completion` = NULL (optional)
- **Transformation location note:** WHERE clause performs extraction filtering only. All business logic remains in Spotfire.
- **This addresses root causes:** #1, #2, #8.

### Step 6 — Configure Document Properties as Filter Parameters
- **Menu path:** `Edit > Document Properties > Properties` tab.
- Create document properties:
  - `dp_StartDate` (Date, default = 7 days ago)
  - `dp_EndDate` (Date, default = today)
  - `dp_Reservoir` (String)
  - `dp_Field` (String)
  - `dp_PipeNetwork` (String)
  - `dp_EngString` (String, allow blank)
  - `dp_Completion` (String, allow blank)
- Bind each SQL `:Parameter` to its corresponding document property in the data connection configuration.
- **Audit note:** Document each property name, type, default, and bound SQL parameter.

### Step 7 — Build Cascading Filter UI (Property Controls from Control Plane)
- Create a dedicated "Filter Selection" text area (top of main page or dedicated page).
- **Menu path:** Insert property controls via `Insert > Property Control` in the Text Area.
- Layout in cascade order, each driven by the Control-Plane DimensionLookup table:
  1. **Date Range:** Drop-down with options: 3 Days / 7 Days / 1 Month / 1 Quarter / 6 Months. Sets `dp_StartDate`.
  2. **Reservoir:** Drop-down (Diatomite / Tulare). Values from DimensionLookup.
  3. **Field:** Drop-down filtered by selected Reservoir (~4 options). Values from DimensionLookup.
  4. **Pipe/Network:** Drop-down filtered by Field (~20 options). Values from DimensionLookup.
  5. **Engineering String:** Drop-down filtered by Pipe/Network (~80 options). Optional.
  6. **Completion/Well:** Drop-down filtered upstream (~300 options). Optional.
- Add a **"Load Data"** action button (`Insert > Action Control > Button`) that triggers data table refresh on the Fact-Plane table.
- **Decision branch:**
  - IF any of first four selections (Date, Reservoir, Field, Pipe/Network) are blank → display: *"Please select Date Range, Reservoir, Field, and Pipe/Network before loading data."* → disable Load Data button.
  - IF all four populated → enable Load Data button.
- **Key advantage of Control-Plane pattern:** Filter drop-downs are populated instantly from the resident DimensionLookup table — no Oracle round-trip for each cascade level. This was not possible in a pure on-demand approach.
- **Transformation location note:** All filter logic is Spotfire UI configuration. DB receives only the parameterized SELECT.

### Step 8 — Optimize Fact-Plane Column Types and Key/Label Split
- In the Fact-Plane query, select only integer/code key columns for dimensions (not full text names).
- Display labels come from the Control-Plane DimensionLookup table via `Data > Column Matches…` or calculated column lookups.
- **Menu path:** `Data > Data Table Properties > Columns` — verify all 50 columns:
  - Dates → `Date` or `DateTime`, not `String`.
  - Numeric facts → `Real` or `Integer`, not `String`.
  - Dimension keys → `Integer` where possible.
- IF a column imports as wrong type → adjust SQL SELECT to explicitly `CAST`, or use `Insert > Transformation > Calculate new column`.
- **This addresses root cause:** #3 (string footprint reduction).
- **Audit note:** Include column type manifest and key/label mapping in documentation.

### Step 9 — Organize Calculated Columns and Conditional Visuals
- Catalog every calculated column:

| Column Name | Formula | Business Meaning | Depends On | Test Case |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| (fill per actual columns) | | | | |

- Move heavy calculated columns to pages/visuals active only after Fact-Plane load completes.
- IF a calc references full table context (e.g., `Over()` across all rows) → evaluate whether it can be scoped to filtered subset.
- **Transformation location note:** All calculations remain in Spotfire.
- **Audit note:** Maintain formula catalog as living document.

### Step 10 — Test in Web Player (Iterative)
- Save to Library: `File > Save As > Library Item…` → increment version.
- Execute test matrix:

| Test | Users | Date Range | Filters Applied | Expected Outcome |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| T1 | 1 | 7 days | Reservoir + Field + Pipe | Opens < 15s (Control Plane only), fact load < 10s |
| T2 | 1 | 1 month | Reservoir + Field + Pipe + String | Fact load < 20s |
| T3 | 1 | 6 months | All filters applied (narrow scope) | Fact load < 40s |
| T4 | 3 | 7 days each | Various combos | All sessions stable, filter response < 2s |
| T5 | 3 | 1 month each | Various | All sessions stable |

- **Decision branch:**
  - IF any test crashes → reduce default date window or add row-count guard.
  - IF all pass → proceed to final validation.
- **Audit note:** Record all test results with timestamps, user counts, session durations.

### Step 11 — Final Library Publish and Documentation
- Save verified analysis as the promoted production version in Library.
- Ensure the analysis contains (text pages or embedded):
  - Architecture summary (Control-Plane/Fact-Plane design)
  - Footprint estimation record (method used, values, date measured)
  - Parameter register (name, type, default, bound SQL parameter)
  - Field lineage map (Oracle column → Spotfire column → transformation)
  - Control-Plane table inventory (CalendarDates, DimensionLookup: SQL, row counts, purpose)
  - Formula catalog
  - Test evidence summary
  - Change log with Library version history
- **Audit note:** This documentation set constitutes the audit package. Update with each release.

---

## D) Performance Validation

| Metric | Method | Target |
|---|---|---|
| Control-Plane open time | Stopwatch from Web Player URL click to filter UI usable (3 runs, median) | ≤ 15 seconds |
| Fact-Plane gated load time | Stopwatch from "Load Data" click to visuals populated (3 runs, median) | ≤ 20 seconds (7-day scope); ≤ 40 seconds (6-month narrow scope) |
| Filter control responsiveness | Time from drop-down click to options populated (driven by Control Plane) | ≤ 1 second |
| Concurrent session stability | 3 simultaneous Web Player sessions, each performing independent filter/load cycles | Zero crashes over 30-minute test window |
| Memory footprint per session | Method 1 (Spotfire evidence) or Task Manager during Web Player load | Control Plane: < 10 MB; Fact Plane (typical): < 200 MB |
| Total node memory (3 sessions) | Server monitoring or admin console if accessible | < 80% of node RAM |
| Traceability validation | For 3 filter scenarios, compare Spotfire row counts and sampled aggregates to Oracle SELECT with identical WHERE predicates | Exact row-count match; aggregates within rounding tolerance |
| Documentation validation | Review checklist: all items in Step 11 present and current | All items present and internally consistent |

---

## E) Risks and Mitigations

| # | Risk | Category | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Undocumented parameter defaults challenged in audit | **Audit** | Medium | High | Maintain controlled parameter register in analysis. Include defaults and rationale in every Library version's change log. |
| 2 | User selects 6-month + broad filters, causing large fact load | Performance | Medium | High | Enforce mandatory upstream filter gates. Consider row-count estimate warning if projected load exceeds 500K rows. |
| 3 | DimensionLookup key/label mismatch after source data changes | Data trust | Low | High | Add reconciliation check (row counts, key-label consistency) to release testing. Flag new keys not in lookup. |
| 4 | No Automation Services → first user of the day experiences cold-start latency | Operations | High | Medium | Control Plane loads in < 15s even cold. Document expected timing for users. |
| 5 | Control-Plane DimensionLookup grows beyond expected ~30K rows | Scalability | Low | Medium | Monitor row count each release. If growth is significant, partition by reservoir or add WHERE filter to dimension query. |
| 6 | Footprint estimate (Method 3) proves inaccurate after Method 1 measurement | Planning | Medium | Medium | Collect Method 1 evidence at first opportunity. Revise architecture parameters if actual footprint differs by >30%. |

---

## F) Spotfire Administration Scope

- The core solution **does not require** any Spotfire Server administrative changes.
- No modifications to Web Player node memory limits, service configurations, trust settings, or hardware are assumed.
- IF performance targets in Section D are not met after full implementation, the following **out-of-scope future enhancements** could be evaluated (requires server admin involvement):
  - Increase per-session memory allocation on Web Player nodes.
  - Add Web Player node capacity for horizontal scaling.
  - Implement Automation Services scheduled cache refresh (requires licensing/configuration).
- These are documented for completeness only. They are **not part of the recommended solution**.

---

## G) Scripting and Advanced Extensions

- The primary solution is **entirely configuration-based**: parameterized queries, document properties, property controls, action controls, data table configuration, and standard Spotfire features.
- **No IronPython scripts, R/Python Data Functions, or TERR scripts are required.**
- IF a future requirement cannot be met through configuration alone (e.g., dynamic row-count estimation before load), a minimal IronPython script could be introduced. In that case:
  - The configuration-first alternative must be documented alongside it.
  - The script must include inline comments explaining purpose and logic.
  - The script must be stored in the Library analysis and version-controlled.
- At this time, no such requirement exists.

---

## H) Final Checklist

| # | Checkpoint | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Accuracy:** Row counts and sampled aggregates match Oracle source for ≥ 3 filter scenarios | ☐ Verify during Step 10 |
| 2 | **Performance:** Control Plane open ≤ 15s, fact load ≤ 20–40s, filters ≤ 1s, zero crashes at 3 concurrent sessions | ☐ Verify during Step 10 |
| 3 | **Traceability:** Every column mapped Oracle source → Spotfire field → calculated column (if any) | ☐ Verify in lineage map |
| 4 | **Transformation location:** All business calculations documented as Spotfire-side; DB performs extraction only | ☐ Verify in architecture summary |
| 5 | **Footprint estimation:** Method used, values, and unknowns documented; Method 1 evidence collected when available | ☐ Verify in footprint record |
| 6 | **Control-Plane inventory:** CalendarDates and DimensionLookup tables documented (SQL, row counts, purpose) | ☐ Verify in table inventory |
| 7 | **Parameter register:** All document properties, defaults, and SQL bindings documented | ☐ Verify in parameter register |
| 8 | **Formula catalog:** All calculated columns cataloged with name, formula, owner, meaning, test case | ☐ Verify in formula catalog |
| 9 | **Test evidence:** All diagnostic and validation test results recorded with timestamps | ☐ Verify in test log |
| 10 | **Library versioning:** Analysis saved to Library with version history and change descriptions | ☐ Verify in Library |
| 11 | **Capability constraints respected:** No Oracle DDL, no Automation Services, no server admin changes, no scripting in core path | ☐ Confirmed via Internal Self-Audit table above |
| 12 | **Customer profile satisfied:** Transparent, reproducible, traceable, defensible, audit-ready | ☐ Confirmed by architecture design principles |
